query: Is the audience 'Email Opt-In' referenced in more than one journey simultaneously?
normalized_query: Is the audience 'Email Opt-In' referenced in more than one journey simultaneously?

query_analysis.reason: The query requires first identifying the 'Email Opt-In' audience and then finding all active journeys associated with it to determine if there are multiple simultaneous references.

subqueries.query: List all active journeys referencing the 'Email Opt-In' audience

route.reason: Selected from hybrid retrieval route votes with conservative domain keyword overrides.

domain.reason: Selected from top retrieved examples and domain votes.

openapi.description: Returns a list of journeys based on the provided filters.

query_params.name: filter
query_params.description: Search filters in URL-encoded format. Supports &-separated fields with basic operators '=', '>', '<', '>=', '<='.

query_params.name: page
query_params.description: Page number for pagination (0-based). Used to navigate through multiple pages of results.

query_params.name: pageSize
query_params.description: Number of items per page. Controls how many journeys are returned in a single response.

query_params.name: fields
query_params.description: Comma-separated list of fields to include in the response. Allows selective field retrieval for performance optimization.

query_params.name: sort
query_params.description: Sort criteria in format 'field=direction'. Multiple sorts can be comma-separated. Direction can be 'asc' or 'desc'.

header_params.name: x-api-key
header_params.description: The API key belonging to the calling client. Use the client id from Developer Console.

header_params.name: x-gw-ims-org-id
header_params.description: The IMS org id for which the action is being taken.

matched_subqueries.subquery: List all active journeys referencing the 'Email Opt-In' audience

similar_examples.gold_sql:
SELECT J.CAMPAIGNID AS campaign_id,
       J.NAME AS campaign_name,
       J.STATE AS campaign_state,
       J.UPDATEDTIME AS updated_time
FROM DIM_CAMPAIGN AS J
WHERE LOWER(J.STATE) NOT IN ('deployed', 'redeployed')
LIMIT 50

matched_subqueries.subquery: List all active journeys referencing the 'Email Opt-In' audience

similar_examples.gold_sql:
SELECT CAMPAIGN.NAME AS CAMPAIGNNAME,
       CAMPAIGN.CAMPAIGNID
FROM DIM_CAMPAIGN AS CAMPAIGN
LIMIT 10

similar_examples.query: List all segment audiences connected to the destination named 'SMS Opt-In', showing audienceId, name, totalProfiles, createdTime, updatedTime, and used in other audience count for each audience. Remove any row limit from the results.
similar_examples.gold_sql:
SELECT 
    a.segmentid AS segment_id,
    a.name AS segment_name,
    a.totalmembers AS total_profiles,
    a.createdTime AS created_time,
    a.updatedTime AS updated_time
FROM dim_segment a
JOIN hkg_br_segment_target ad ON a.segmentid = ad.segmentid
JOIN dim_target d ON ad.targetId = d.targetId
WHERE d.dataflowName = 'SMS Opt-In'
ORDER BY a.name

similar_examples.query: When was the journey 'Birthday Message' published?
similar_examples.gold_sql:
SELECT name AS campaign_name,
       lastdeployedtime AS published_time
FROM dim_campaign
WHERE name IN ('Birthday Message', 'Gold Tier Welcome Email')
LIMIT 50

similar_examples.query: Provide more details for the schema 'weRetail: Customer Actions'
similar_examples.gold_sql:
SELECT S.BLUEPRINTID AS blueprint_id,
       S.NAME,
       S.CLASS,
       S.ISPROFILEENABLED,
       S.UPDATEDTIME AS updated_time,
       S.REQUIREDFIELDS AS required_fields,
       COUNT(DISTINCT SD.COLLECTIONID) AS collection_count,
       COUNT(DISTINCT SA.PROPERTY) AS property_count
FROM DIM_BLUEPRINT AS S
LEFT JOIN HKG_BR_BLUEPRINT_COLLECTION AS SD ON S.BLUEPRINTID = SD.BLUEPRINTID
LEFT JOIN HKG_BR_BLUEPRINT_PROPERTY AS SA ON S.BLUEPRINTID = SA.BLUEPRINTID
WHERE LOWER(S.NAME) = LOWER('weRetail: Customer Actions')
GROUP BY S.BLUEPRINTID,
         S.NAME,
         S.CLASS,
         S.ISPROFILEENABLED,
         S.UPDATEDTIME,
         S.REQUIREDFIELDS
LIMIT 3

answer_constraints: Use only verified SQL/API outputs as evidence.
Do not invent IDs, timestamps, counts, statuses, or names.
If SQL and API disagree, mention the discrepancy clearly.
If API returns an error or empty result, state that clearly.
